---
title: "Solution 2A — Microbiome import and EDA"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-tools: true
---

**Instructor notes:** ~282 samples after join. PCA separation Early/Late is often visible but not perfect. Students may use CLR or log1p — both acceptable if documented.

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
theme_set(theme_minimal())
```

```{r load}
source("_load_microbiome.R")
mic <- load_microbiome()
otu_cols <- mic_otu_cols(mic)
df <- mic |> select(sample_id, Label, Sex, Day, Individual)
otu_f <- as.matrix(mic[, otu_cols])
rownames(otu_f) <- mic$sample_id
dim(otu_f)
table(df$Label)
ncol(otu_f)
```

```{r library-size, fig.width=7, fig.height=4}
df$lib_size <- rowSums(otu_f)
ggplot(df, aes(lib_size, fill = Label)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, position = "identity", alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(title = "Library size after prevalence filter", x = "Total counts (log scale)")
```

```{r pca, fig.width=7, fig.height=5}
log_otu <- log1p(otu_f)
pc <- prcomp(log_otu, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)
pc_df <- as.data.frame(pc$x[, 1:2]) |>
  mutate(sample_id = rownames(otu_f)) |>
  left_join(df, by = "sample_id")
ggplot(pc_df, aes(PC1, PC2, color = Label, shape = Sex)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "PCA of log1p OTU abundances")
```

```{r session, echo=FALSE}
sessionInfo()
```
